option.name: place-spi-master-driver-in-ram
option.description: Places the SPI master driver in RAM for better performance

option.name: place-switch-tables-in-ram
option.description: Places switch-tables, some lookup tables and constants related to interrupt handling into RAM - resulting in better performance but slightly more RAM consumption.

option.name: place-anon-in-ram
option.description: Places anonymous symbols into RAM - resulting in better performance at the cost of significant more RAM consumption. Best to be combined with `place-switch-tables-in-ram`.

option.name: xtal-frequency
option.description: The frequency of the crystal oscillator, in MHz. Set to `auto` to automatically detect the frequency. `auto` may not be able to identify the clock frequency in some cases. Also, configuring a specific frequency may increase performance slightly.

option.name: spi-address-workaround
option.description: Enables a workaround for the issue where SPI in half-duplex mode incorrectly transmits the address on a single line if the data buffer is empty.

option.name: flip-link
option.description: Move the stack to start of RAM to get zero-cost stack overflow protection.

option.name: stack-guard-offset
option.description: The stack guard variable will be placed this many bytes from the stack's end.

option.name: stack-guard-value
option.description: The value to be written to the stack guard variable.

option.name: impl-critical-section
option.description: Provide a `critical-section` implementation. Note that if disabled, you will need to provide a `critical-section` implementation which is using `restore-state-u32`.
